# MIRA AdultEdge — llms.txt
# Machine-readable project summary for AI coding assistants

## Identity
- Name: MIRA AdultEdge
- Type: Judgment-bias diagnostic agent — original PSR theory base with cognitive-science anchors
- Version: 0.2.5 (alpha)
- License: MIT (code) + CC BY 4.0 (theory/design) + CC BY-NC 4.0 (research/howstudy guide corpus)
- Python: 3.12+

## What It Does
MIRA analyzes learner-produced text to identify judgment biases using a 3-stage PSR framework (Premise -> Strategy -> Result) and 3-lane verification (Critic + Prolog + LLM). It provides structured diagnostic reports with non-directive coaching suggestions.

## Architecture
- System A: Diagnostic engine — 3-axis analysis (metacognition, task definition, learning stage)
- System B: Pattern detection — 3-lane verification (Lane 1: Rule-based critic checks, Lane 2: Prolog formal rules, Lane 3: LLM candidate extraction)
- Pipeline: A->B->A loop — System A diagnoses, System B verifies patterns, System A generates coaching
- 21 judgment distortion patterns defined (6 with dedicated coaching content)

## Key Files
- mira/pipeline.py — Main orchestration loop
- mira/report.py — Report generation with i18n (en/ko)
- mira/psr/psr.py — PSR decomposition and vtype routing
- mira/system_a/engine/ — Diagnostic engine (3 axes)
- mira/system_b/engine/ — Pattern matcher + critic
- mira/contracts/minimal.py — Type contracts (Pydantic v2)
- spec/shared/pattern_registry.yaml — Pattern definitions (SSOT)
- spec/shared/enums.yaml — Cognitive domain enumerations
- spec/shared/invariants.yaml — Safety invariants
- research/howstudy/ — Adult-learning guide corpus behind Domain Pack v1 (complete English edition + Korean core + 2003 origin documents)

## Entry Points
- CLI: python -m mira "text" (--lang en/ko, --format text/json)
- Python: from mira.pipeline import run_minimal_loop

## Testing
- 500+ tests, all deterministic (no live API calls)
- pytest: python -m pytest

## Bilingual
- Input: English + Korean learner text supported
- Output: English default, --lang ko for Korean
- Korean regex patterns in code are intentional NLP features

## Constraints
- No verdict language in output (enforced by contract)
- No willpower/blame language (structural reattribution only)
- LLM output restricted to 5 admissible tags
- External LLM use is opt-in (MIRA_ENABLE_LANE3) — without it the system runs Lane 1 fully; Lane 2 reports unverified
